pattern: Because every value is bounded, a fixed-size frequency table over the negative range turns 'find the x-th smallest in this window' into a cheap scan instead of a re-sort on every slide.
hints: Track a running count of how many times each possible negative value currently appears inside the window, updating it as the window slides by one on each side.
To read off the x-th smallest negative, walk the frequency table from the most negative value upward, subtracting counts until you've covered x occurrences.
If a window doesn't contain at least x negative numbers, its beauty is defined to be 0 — don't let the scan run past what's actually there.

edge_inputs.description: window size 1, beauty is just the element or 0
some windows have fewer negatives than x
repeated identical negative values in a window
x equals k, asking for the most negative value in the window
values at the extremes of the allowed range

stress.description: random values across the full negative/positive range with random window size and rank
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-50, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    x = rng.randint(1, k)
    return [nums, k, x]
stress.note: x is always drawn within [1, k] so it stays a meaningful rank for the window size
